output.code:
# Let's open the uploaded text file to analyze the content and compute offsets.
import os, json, re, textwrap, sys, unicodedata, itertools
path = "/mnt/data/file-ML6YMEfTqWQw4hgXKfqNcu-Q076.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]

# Let's search for likely citation phrases and record their spans.
s = text

candidates = [
    "فَلَمَّا ذَهَبُوا",  # start of 12:15 phrase
    "لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُمْ", # segment of 12:15
    "وَهُمْ لاَ يَشْعُرُونَ", # tail of 12:15 with nonstandard alef
    "وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ", # variation a bit later
    "فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا", # start of 12:88
    "وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْهِ", # present in first quote
    "وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْهِ", # variant in [2]
    "وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْهِ", # variant in [3]
]
spans = []
for pat in candidates:
    start = 0
    while True:
        idx = s.find(pat, start)
        if idx == -1:
            break
        # expand to capture to the end of Arabic segment or closing quote/paren/period
        # But we want the exact quoted segment; we'll try to capture until we hit a closing punctuation that indicates end of quote.
        # We'll attempt to find the nearest ending char among ["\"", ")", ".", "\n", "…", "]"] after idx
        # but since we want accurate spans of the explicit citations, we might need custom handling for each context.
        spans.append((pat, idx))
        start = idx + 1

len(spans), spans[:20]

# Find double quoted segments
dq_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    dq_spans.append((m.start(1), m.end(1), m.group(1)))
len(dq_spans), dq_spans[:5]

# Find parentheses segments
paren_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'\(([^)]+)\)', text):
    paren_spans.append((m.start(1), m.end(1), m.group(1)))
len(paren_spans)

start_idx = 3809
next_dot = text.find('.', start_idx)
start_idx, next_dot, text[start_idx:next_dot]

start_awo7yna = 3958
end_dot = text.find('.', start_awo7yna)
text_context = text[start_awo7yna: end_dot]
end_dot, text_context[:200]

queries: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبُوا بِهِ وَأَجْمَعُوا أَن يَجْعَلُوهُ فِي غَيَابَةِ ٱلْجُبِّ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْهِ لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُم بِأَمْرِهِمْ هَـٰذَا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ قَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَا الْعَزِيزُ مَسَّنَا وَأَهْلَنَا الضُّرُّ (يوسف 88)

output.code:
# Build the selected spans with start,end 
selected_spans = []

# 1) First double-quoted verse
span1 = (dq_spans[0][0], dq_spans[0][1])  # start,end
selected_spans.append(span1)

# 2) Parentheses full verse (trim spaces)
p2 = paren_spans[1]  # 2234-2380
s2_start, s2_end = p2[0], p2[1]
# trim spaces
while s2_start < s2_end and text[s2_start].isspace():
    s2_start += 1
while s2_end > s2_start and text[s2_end-1].isspace():
    s2_end -= 1
selected_spans.append((s2_start, s2_end))

# 3) Partial ""( لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُمْ ... )"
p3 = paren_spans[2]
s3_start, s3_end = p3[0], p3[1]
while s3_start < s3_end and text[s3_start].isspace():
    s3_start += 1
while s3_end > s3_start and text[s3_end-1].isspace():
    s3_end -= 1
selected_spans.append((s3_start, s3_end))

# 4) Partial "( وَهُمْ لاَ يَشْعُرُونَ )"
p4 = paren_spans[3]
s4_start, s4_end = p4[0], p4[1]
while s4_start < s4_end and text[s4_start].isspace():
    s4_start += 1
while s4_end > s4_start and text[s4_end-1].isspace():
    s4_end -= 1
selected_spans.append((s4_start, s4_end))

# 5) "( فَلَمَّا دَخَلُواْ علَيْهِ ... الضر" phrase until dot
start_12_88 = 3809
end_12_88 = text.find('.', start_12_88)
phrase_12_88 = text[start_12_88:end_12_88].rstrip()
# adjust end to remove trailing spaces
end_12_88_trim = start_12_88 + len(phrase_12_88)
selected_spans.append((start_12_88, end_12_88_trim))

# 6) "وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْهِ لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُمْ ... وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ"
start6 = 3958
end6 = 4037
selected_spans.append((start6, end6))

# Build list of dicts
span_texts = [(a,b,text[a:b]) for (a,b) in selected_spans]
len(span_texts), span_texts[:10]

# Prepare canonical texts as obtained via file_search results (copied carefully).
canonical_12_15 = "فَلَمَّا ذَهَبُوا بِهِ وَأَجْمَعُوا أَنْ يَجْعَلُوهُ فِي غَيَابَتِ الْجُبِّ ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْهِ لَتُنَبِّئَنَّهُمْ بِأَمْرِهِمْ هَـٰذَا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ"
canonical_12_88 = "فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ قَالُوا يَا أَيُّهَا الْعَزِيزُ مَسَّنَا وَأَهْلَنَا الضُّرُّ وَجِئْنَا بِبِضَاعَةٍ مُزْجَاةٍ فَأَوْفِ لَنَا الْكَيْلَ وَتَصَدَّقْ عَلَيْنَا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَجْزِي الْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ"

# Determine is_valid for each span (compare to canonical; must match entire ayah)
def is_valid_span(span_text):
    return span_text == canonical_12_15 or span_text == canonical_12_88

validities = [is_valid_span(t) for _,_,t in span_texts]
validities, [t for _,_,t in span_texts if is_valid_span(t)]

# Build the final citations list
citations = []

for (start, end, t) in span_texts:
    if "فَلَمَّا دَخَلُوا" in t:
        corrected = canonical_12_88
        sura_name = "يوسف"
        sura_number = 12
        aya_number = 88
    else:
        corrected = canonical_12_15
        sura_name = "يوسف"
        sura_number = 12
        aya_number = 15
    item = {
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": t,
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": t == corrected,
        "aya_reference": {
            "sura_name": sura_name,
            "sura_number": sura_number,
            "aya_number": aya_number
        },
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": corrected if corrected else "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": False if corrected else True
    }
    citations.append(item)

# Self-check: verify corrected_text equals canonical text for assigned sura/ayah
# We'll assert equality for all
all_equal = []
for c in citations:
    if c["aya_reference"]["aya_number"] == 15:
        all_equal.append(c["corrected_text"] == canonical_12_15)
    else:
        all_equal.append(c["corrected_text"] == canonical_12_88)
all_equal

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
